story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has temporarily lifted the interest rate ceiling on select deposits, including FCNR(B) and NRE deposits, to allow banks to offer higher interest rates to Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) and Persons of Indian Origin (PIOs). This move aims to mobilize foreign exchange. The relaxation is in effect until September 30.

articles.title: [Maharashtra Ownership Flats Act] Second Deemed Conveyance Application Barred If Earlier Rejection Has Attained Finality: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that where an earlier order rejecting a deemed conveyance application has attained finality, a second application seeking the same relief would be barred by principles of res judicata and finality of proceedings. The Court observed that the Competent Authority under the Maharashtra Ownership Flats Act does not possess any inherent power of review and cannot reopen or reconsider an issue already decided unless such power is expressly conferred by statute. The decline extended a sharp selloff from the previous session, taking silver’s two-day loss to ₹20,000 per kg and gold’s to ₹7,000 per 10 grams.

The tribunal set aside the customs duty demand, redemption fine, and penalties. This ruling has significant implications for trade and finance in India.
articles.raw_summary: CESTAT held that the exporter established complete correlation between the exported, re-imported and re-exported frozen buffalo meat through documentary evidence and departmental supervision. It set aside the customs duty demand, redemption fine and penalties after finding compliance with Notification No. 158/95-Cus. articles.title: India to allow import of 3.78 lakh UK cars at concessional duty in first 15 years of trade pact
articles.summary: India will allow imports of up to 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional duties under a new trade pact starting July 15. Import duties will fall from 110% to 10% with quotas, while Indian electric and hybrid cars will gain duty-free access to the UK market from the sixth year.

articles.title: Delhi High Court to rule on Telegram's challenge to India-wide block on Friday
articles.summary: During the hearing on Thursday, the Centre defended the blocking order, arguing that Telegram cannot invoke the principle of proportionality if it is unable to ensure accountability on its platform. The Centre argued that Telegram's architecture enables cybercrime and the circulation of leaked examination papers, while the company said the move is disproportionate and affects around 150 million users.

The Centre argued that Telegram's architecture enables cybercrime and the circulation of leaked examination papers, while the company said the move is disproportionate and affects around 150 million users.

articles.title: Supervisory Data Quality Index for Scheduled Commercial Banks  (March 2026)
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released the Supervisory Data Quality Index (sDQI) for SCBs for March 2026 . It measures data quality in terms of the Accuracy, Timeliness, Completeness and Consistency in the submission of returns and microdata for supervisory assessments. The objective of sDQI is to assess the adherence to the principles enunciated in the Master Direction on Filing of Supervisory Returns 2024 . (Brij Raj)             Chief General Manager Press Release: 2026-2027/493

articles.title: Danger Of Judge Made Intra-Court Appeals
articles.summary: The Supreme Court's power to revisit its own orders is causing concern. The issue is not whether the Court can correct itself, but whether it should reopen its own orders in response to social media outrage. This is a governance and legal issue that requires careful consideration.
articles.raw_summary: In a constitutional democracy, the Supreme Court commands obedience not because it is infallible, but because it is institutionally disciplined. Its power is not the power to react; it is the power to decide according to law. That is why any emerging tendency of the Court to revisit, neutralise, or effectively reopen its own orders in the wake of social media outrage should cause serious concern. articles.title: India’s fossil fuel insecurity is now a systems risk, not just an import-dependence problem
articles.summary: India’s energy security challenge is no longer just about how much coal, oil, and gas the country imports. The deeper risk is that India’s fossil fuel system is exposed across the board — through concentrated suppliers, vulnerable shipping routes, limited reserves and storage, refinery constraints, and direct exposure to global price volatility.

articles.title: Govt to replace WPI with Producer Price Index, new series from June 15
articles.summary: The Indian government will replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with the Producer Price Index (PPI) starting June 15. This change aims to better measure price changes at the producer level. The WPI is currently used to track average price changes in a basket of commodities. The shift to PPI reflects an update in economic indicators used for policy and analysis.
articles.raw_summary: WPI is a key indicator for measuring the average change in the prices of a fixed basket of commodities at the initial stage of a commercial transaction over a given period, with reference to a base year.

Analysts anticipate further moderation and a shift towards M A and new client acquisition to counter slowing growth.

The court seeks supporting documents for the blocking order, while Telegram faces restrictions until June 30, with the app delisted from app stores.

articles.title: India-UK trade deal to offer export benefits from day-one; steel issues addressed: Govt Sources
articles.summary: The landmark India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will come into force from July 15, 2026, with the government working to ensure that customs notifications and related processes are put in place so that exporters can start availing concessions from day one, senior government sources told ANI.

articles.title: RBI not in favour of offshore settlement for government ​securities, sources say
articles.summary: The RBI is steering clear of offshore settlement for government securities, preferring foreign investors to trade directly on the domestic NDS-OM platform. Despite recent tax incentives, the RBI aims to consolidate liquidity locally for better price discovery and ease of trading, ensuring a unified market.
articles.raw_summary: The RBI is steering clear of offshore settlement for government securities, preferring foreign investors to trade directly on the domestic NDS-OM platform. Despite recent tax incentives, the RBI aims to consolidate liquidity locally for better price discovery and ease of trading, ensuring a unified market.

articles.title: CAG asks states to disclose off-budget borrowings in A/Cs
articles.summary: India's Comptroller and Auditor General, K Sanjay Murthy, urged state governments to fully disclose their off-budget borrowings. He highlighted that these liabilities have reached ₹90.51 lakh crore. States are advised to reduce these debts over time. Accurate reporting is crucial for understanding the true scale of state finances and ensuring fiscal transparency. This will aid informed decision-making by policymakers.
articles.raw_summary: India's Comptroller and Auditor General, K Sanjay Murthy, urged state governments to fully disclose their off-budget borrowings. He highlighted that these liabilities have reached ₹90.51 lakh crore. States are advised to reduce these debts over time. Accurate reporting is crucial for understanding the true scale of state finances and ensuring fiscal transparency. This will aid informed decision-making by policymakers.

articles.title: Finmin to engage with states over looming El Nino fallout
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry will soon meet with states and central ministries to assess the economic and fiscal impact of El Nino. This review will focus on potential stress on agriculture, food inflation, and increased subsidy outgo. The government is preparing relief packages for vulnerable districts. This proactive approach aims to ensure adequate supplies and mitigate inflation spikes.
articles.raw_summary: The Finance Ministry will soon meet with states and central ministries to assess the economic and fiscal impact of El Nino. This review will focus on potential stress on agriculture, food inflation, and increased subsidy outgo. The government is preparing relief packages for vulnerable districts. This proactive approach aims to ensure adequate supplies and mitigate inflation spikes.

articles.title: Sebi board to consider reintroducing open-market window for buybacks on Friday
articles.summary: Markets regulator Sebi's board is set to meet on Friday to deliberate on a wideranging agenda, including a proposal to reintroduce openmarket buybacks, faster clearance for AIF schemes and relaxed intraday borrowing rules for mutual funds, people familiar with the matter said.

articles.summary: Devyani International and Sapphire Foods shares rose up to 7% after the BSE gave its nod for their merger proposal. The companies can now file the merger proposal before the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT). This development is significant for the companies and their investors.

articles.title: Real test of AI regulations: Reducing pendency of court cases
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has released a draft of AI Regulations, which goes beyond existing precedents from other countries. The draft aims to address critical areas related to AI. The court's move is significant as it sets the stage for further discussions and potential policy changes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court’s draft AI Regulations go well beyond existing precedents from other countries. But a few critical areas still need addressing.

articles.title: Fuel curbs rollback likely as Iran deal raises hope
articles.summary: The Centre is considering lifting emergency curbs on fuel sales, which could ease pressure on farmers and lower inflation. This move comes after the US and Iran sued for peace. The decision could improve prospects for India, the world's third-largest oil importer.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre is weighing a rollback of emergency curbs on fuel sales imposed during the West Asia conflict after the US and Iran sued for peace, a move that could ease pressure on farmers, lower inflation and improve prospects for the world's third-largest oil importer.

articles.title: Net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to  ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June
articles.summary: Net direct tax collections in India rose 14.6% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June, driven by a 22.5% surge in corporate tax receipts and a near-45% jump in STT collections. This increase is significant for the Indian economy, but it does not directly imply any new policy or action.
articles.raw_summary: Net direct tax collections rose 14.64% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June, led by a 22.5% surge in corporate tax receipts and a near-45% jump in STT collections.

articles.title: Basmati rice exports to West Asia poised for rebound after US, Iran sign peace deal
articles.summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.
articles.raw_summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.

articles.title: As war pressures ebb, edible oil prices may ease 10% ahead of the festive season
articles.summary: India could see edible oil prices fall by up to 10% in coming months as easing West Asia tensions, lower freight costs and higher palm oil supplies from Indonesia and Malaysia improve availability. However, benefits may take time to reach consumers due to existing inventories and supply contracts.
articles.raw_summary: India could see edible oil prices fall by up to 10% in coming months as easing West Asia tensions, lower freight costs and higher palm oil supplies from Indonesia and Malaysia improve availability. However, benefits may take time to reach consumers due to existing inventories and supply contracts.

articles.title: With Iran-US peace deal in sight, IEA projects oil supply surplus in 2027
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) projects a global oil supply surplus in 2027, following a disruption in West Asia. This may impact India's energy policy. The IEA expects a 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) decrease in oil supply this year, followed by an 8 million bpd rebound next year.
articles.raw_summary: Global crude oil supply is set to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) to 102.4 million bpd this year amid trade disruptions due to the West Asia war, before rebounding by 8 million bpd next year, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Wednesday.

articles.title: Crude price fall may not last as nations rush to rebuild reserves
articles.summary: Global crude oil prices may not plummet due to a supply shortfall, despite a potential US-Iran peace deal. This could have implications for India's energy policy. The article highlights the complex factors influencing global oil prices.
articles.raw_summary: Even with a US-Iran peace deal on the horizon, global crude oil prices are not expected to plummet due to a significant supply shortfall. nbsp;

articles.title: States' liabilities rise to  ₹90.5 tn in FY25, many breach fiscal gap limit: CAG
articles.summary: As of 31 March 2025, the total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. This reflects growing borrowing requirements across states, with public debt more than tripling over the past decade.
articles.raw_summary: As of 31 March 2025, total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. Public debt has more than tripled over the past decade, rising from ₹23.92 trillion in 2015-16, reflecting growing borrowing requirements across states.

articles.title: Shipping lines stay cautious even after US-Iran peace deal, await security clarity before resuming Gulf operations

articles.title: Digvijaya backs Priyank Kharge call for RSS funding disclosure, says he’d written to Bhagwat, Nirmala too
articles.summary: Congress leader Digvijaya Singh has called for the RSS to disclose its funding, saying it's essential for transparency in a democracy. He claims to have written to RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat and Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman on the issue. This demand comes as part of a broader debate on the role of the RSS in Indian society.

articles.title: International Finance Corporation announces $371 million investment in data centre operator Sify
articles.summary: The International Finance Corporation is investing $371 million in Sify Technologies. This funding will develop two new data centres in Navi Mumbai and Chennai. This move is set to boost India's digital transformation and infrastructure.

articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz is significantly leveraging its Indian R D center in Bengaluru for its biggest software transformation to date, MB.OS, which will power future S-Class models and other vehicles. articles.title: Fintechs bet big on corporate bonds as retail participation surges
articles.summary: Retail investors are increasingly participating in corporate bonds, with monthly investments surging to Rs 1,500-2,000 crore. Fintech platforms are actively expanding their offerings in this segment, recognizing bonds as a significant growth opportunity.

articles.title: Tata Electronics onboards Malaysian cleanroom specialist IAQ Group for Dholera fab
articles.summary: Tata Electronics has partnered with IAQ Group to ensure its upcoming semiconductor fab in Dholera meets stringent cleanroom standards. This collaboration aims to ensure timely execution and de-risk the cleanroom readiness bottleneck. The partnership is crucial for the facility's development.

articles.title: Electronics now third largest export category, next target second rank: Ashwini Vaishnaw
articles.summary: India's electronics exports are surging, now its third-largest category, with ambitions to become second. The nation's electronics manufacturing sector is valued at Rs 13 lakh crore. The minister was speaking at the inauguration of American electronics manufacturing services firm Jabil’s new advanced facility in Pune.

articles.title: Jio denies any role in BGP hijacking after Telegram CEO Pavel Durov allegations
articles.summary: Reliance Jio has denied allegations of sabotaging Telegram access outside India. Telegram CEO Pavel Durov claimed BGP hijacking was used. Experts note ambiguity in Durov's statements, with one mentioning Reliance Communications (AS18101) and a snapshot showing the same. Reliance Jio Infocomm (AS55836) was not named in the snapshot. Jio states it follows global best practices for network operations.

Jio states it follows global best practices for network operations.

articles.title: Telegram challenges India block, saying it will hurt free speech rights
articles.summary: Telegram has taken a stand against India's temporary shutdown of its app in the courtroom, a decision enacted to prevent exam leaks. The messaging service contends that this ban undermines users' freedom of expression and violates their constitutional rights. Telegram argues that disabling the entire app is an unwarranted action.

articles.title: CSEZ expands footprint with 58 special economic zones, two more in the pipeline
articles.summary: The Cochin Special Economic Zone has established 58 special economic zones in Karnataka and Kerala, boosting exports and creating thousands of technology jobs. This move is expected to have a significant impact on India's economy. The government's action on special economic zones is a major development in the country's economic landscape.
articles.raw_summary: Over the past decade, the Cochin Special Economic Zone has significantly boosted exports and created thousands of technology jobs by establishing 39 SEZs in Karnataka and 19 in Kerala.

articles.title: Despite West Asia crisis, net direct tax mopup grows 15% to Rs 5.2 lakh crore
articles.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant rise of over 14 percent. This growth is driven by strong advance tax payments from corporations. This indicates robust corporate earnings and a positive business outlook for the current fiscal year. Market activity also remains high, contributing to tax revenues. The collections have resumed their growth path, overcoming previous year's disruptions.

This indicates robust corporate earnings and a positive business outlook for the current fiscal year. Market activity also remains high, contributing to tax revenues. The collections have resumed their growth path, overcoming previous year's disruptions.

articles.title: EPF interest rate retained at 8.25% for FY26
articles.summary: The EPF interest rate for 2025-26 has been set at 8.25%. This rate remains unchanged for the third year. Over 70 million EPF members will see the interest credited by month-end.

articles.title: Peace in West Asia expected to boost order book of Indian exporters: Industry body FIEO
articles.summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) said that the conflict in West Asia suppressed India's trade volume, with exports to the Middle East suffering a sharp decline during the peak of the disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) said that the conflict in West Asia suppressed India's trade volume, with exports to the Middle East suffering a sharp decline during the peak of the disruptions.

articles.title: India's net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to Rs 5.21 lakh crore till June 17
articles.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant jump. Net collections reached Rs 5.21 lakh crore by June 17, marking a 14.64% increase. This growth is fueled by strong corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax payments also show a healthy upward trend, indicating robust income and profitability for taxpayers this financial year.

articles.title: Will continue to support leveraging trade pact provisions, says UK India Business Council
articles.summary: India and the UK are set to implement their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement on July 15. The UK India Business Council will assist its members in understanding and utilizing the pact's benefits. This landmark deal aims to boost trade and investment between the two nations. Focus areas include digital trade, services, manufacturing, education, and innovation.

Focus areas include digital trade, services, manufacturing, education, and innovation.

articles.title: 'Fastest we've ever put a trade deal into force': British High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron hails India-UK trade deal
articles.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will take effect on July 15, 2026, marking the fastest trade deal implementation between the two nations. This historic agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $100-$120 billion by 2030, offering significant economic benefits across sectors like textiles, footwear, and cars.

articles.title: India weighs extending 40-product import duty exemption beyond June 30 amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: The government will decide on extending import duty exemptions for 40 products after June 30. This decision hinges on the evolving situation in West Asia and its impact on cargo movement. Revenue implications for the government are also a key consideration. The current exemption was put in place to support domestic industries facing supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: El Nino fear pushes farmers in Srikakulam and Vizianagaram towards water-saving paddy methods
articles.summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are adopting water-saving rice farming techniques to mitigate the effects of potential El Nino. This shift aims to reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is promoting these changes to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are shifting to water-saving rice farming techniques. Facing potential El Nino impacts, cultivators are embracing Direct Seeded Rice and Alternate Wetting and Drying. These methods significantly reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is actively promoting these changes, alongside soil health improvements, to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

articles.title: UKIBC hails India-UK trade pact rollout from July 15, says CETA to unlock new growth opportunities
articles.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement, CETA, will become effective on July 15, 2026. This landmark deal opens new avenues for trade and investment between India and the United Kingdom. Businesses are encouraged to prepare for its implementation. The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.

The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.

articles.title: Govt ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
articles.summary: The government has approved an 8.25% interest rate on Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) deposits for FY26, clearing the proposal recommended by the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's (EPFO) Central Board of Trustees in March. The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

articles.title: Restoring India’s land, securing India’s future through science and collective action

articles.raw_summary: Scientific innovations and integrated farming systems are combating land degradation and enhancing climate resilience, benefiting millions of farmers and contributing to national development goals.

articles.title: US buyers keen to stitch deals with Indian apparel exporters
articles.summary: Indian apparel exporters face pressure as US buyers demand discounts of up to 10%. This comes as India's readymade garment exports have declined for six consecutive months, largely due to weaker demand in the United States. Competitors like Vietnam and Indonesia are gaining ground.

articles.title: Govt readies farm safety net with banks, insurers as El Nino looms
articles.summary: The Indian government is taking steps to protect rural families from the effects of El Nino on farming. This includes improving loan access, expanding crop insurance, and speeding up claim payouts. Technology like satellite data will be used to support these efforts.
articles.raw_summary: The government is enhancing financial safety nets for rural families. This initiative aims to cushion the impact of El Nino on farming. Efforts include improving loan access, expanding crop insurance, and speeding up claim payouts. Technology like satellite data will be utilized. The Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana is also under review for broader coverage.

articles.title: RBI temporarily withdraws rate caps on FCNR(B), NRE deposits till Sept 30
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has removed interest rate caps on specific non-resident deposits. This move grants banks more freedom to attract foreign funds. The changes are effective immediately and will continue until September 2026. Banks can now offer higher rates on FCNR(B) and NRE deposits. This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

This aims to boost long-term liability mobilisation and improve asset-liability management for financial institutions.

articles.title: UK-India free trade deal to kick in on July 15, cutting tariffs by over $480 mn in year one
articles.summary: The United Kingdom and India will implement their Free Trade Agreement on July 15. This landmark deal, signed last year, promises to boost trade significantly. Businesses have 28 days to prepare for the changes. Tariffs on many UK exports to India will be reduced, including Scotch whisky and automotive products. India will also eliminate tariffs on most UK imports.

India will also eliminate tariffs on most UK imports.

articles.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to do fodder planning to avoid a potential shortage due to El Nino
articles.summary: The Animal Husbandry Department in Maharashtra advises farmers to plan for fodder shortages due to El Nino. This proactive approach aims to prevent a drop in milk production and ensure livestock health during potential feed scarcity from February to June 2027.
articles.raw_summary: Maharashtra farmers are urged to plan for fodder shortages due to El Nino. The Animal Husbandry Department advises cultivating nutritious, multipurpose fodder crops. Farmers should also preserve natural grass and store excess fodder as silage. This proactive approach aims to prevent a drop in milk production and ensure livestock health during potential feed scarcity from February to June 2027.

articles.title: RBI temporarily eases FCNR(B), NRE deposit rate norms to attract overseas funds
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has eased interest rate rules for overseas deposits. This allows banks to offer better returns on FCNR(B) and NRE accounts. The changes aim to attract more foreign funds until September 2026. This move could boost foreign exchange inflows. It will help strengthen the banking system and manage global market volatility.

articles.title: India takes the shine off silver as imports plunge 82% in May after duty hike
articles.summary: May witnessed a steep decline of over 81% in India's silver imports, a result of the government's recent moves to hike import duties and require licenses for incoming shipments. This strategy was prompted by unprecedented levels of silver imports in the last fiscal year, reflecting a clear intent to regulate the market.

articles.raw_summary: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) on June 11 reiterated India's commitment to strengthening Afghanistan's healthcare system, announcing the gifting of medical equipment for diagnosis, treatment, and specialised care to Afghan health authorities.

articles.title: Govt fundraising drive hits high gear
articles.summary: The government's push for non-tax revenue is off to a strong start, with public asset monetisation nearing 30% of last fiscal year's total and disinvestment income already close to its FY26 proceeds. Efforts are being ramped up to exceed the combined FY27 disinvestment and asset monetisation target of ₹80,000 crore.

articles.title: States told to prepare contingency plans for districts likely to face low rainfall during kharif season
articles.summary: India faces its weakest monsoon in 11 years. Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has instructed states to prepare contingency plans for districts likely to experience low or uneven rainfall this kharif season. The government is focusing on water conservation and alternative cropping patterns. Efforts are also underway to boost cotton production through scientific farming.

articles.title: Semiconductor push: CM Naidu invites global tech leaders to invest in AP's Rayalaseema region
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister Naidu has invited global tech leaders to invest in the state's Rayalaseema region, focusing on the semiconductor industry. This move aims to boost the region's economic growth and create new job opportunities. The initiative is part of the state's efforts to attract investments and develop its infrastructure.

articles.title: India Inc's sales growth gathers pace in March quarter, led by autos and metals: RBI data
articles.summary: Manufacturing companies saw sales jump 14.5 percent in the fourth quarter of 2025-26. Automobiles, electrical machinery, and non-ferrous metals led this growth. Overall, listed private non-financial companies achieved double-digit sales growth. Raw material expenses increased, indicating input cost pressure. Staff costs for manufacturing companies moderated. Services sector companies experienced a moderation in operating profit margins.

Services sector companies experienced a moderation in operating profit margins.

articles.title: India, Sri Lanka collaborate to strengthen trade interactions through local currency settlement initiatives
articles.summary: India and Sri Lanka are exploring ways to settle trade using their own currencies. This move aims to cut transaction costs and shield businesses from fluctuating US dollar values. The initiative will help Sri Lanka conserve its dollar reserves. Indian banks can now offer loans in rupees to Sri Lankan importers.

Indian banks can now offer loans in rupees to Sri Lankan importers.

articles.title: States' expenditure rises 131% in 10 yrs on welfare, development: CAG Report
articles.summary: States' spending surged 131 percent from 2015-16 to 2024-25. Welfare and development drove this growth. Revenue expenditure remained the largest budget component. Committed expenses and subsidies consumed over half of revenue spending. Total state expenditure reached Rs 51.20 lakh crore in 2024-25. Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.

Budgetary spending averaged between 15.78 and 17.49 percent of GSDP.

articles.title: India's rice stocks climb to record high; wheat inventories at five-year peak
articles.summary: India's government warehouses hold record rice stocks, up 15% from last year. Wheat inventories are at a five-year peak. These abundant reserves will support increased rice exports. Higher wheat stocks will help manage prices by allowing open market sales. Record production in the last crop year fueled these impressive inventories. India is well-positioned for both exports and domestic price stability.

articles.title: Maharashtra advises farmers to delay sowing as monsoon rains fall short
articles.summary: Maharashtra is experiencing a significant rainfall deficit, with only 26% of its normal rainfall received so far. The government has advised farmers to delay sowing operations. Improved rainfall is anticipated from June 18 onwards in some regions.
articles.raw_summary: Maharashtra is experiencing a significant rainfall deficit in early June. The state has received only 26 percent of its normal rainfall. This has led the government to advise farmers against rushing with sowing operations. Preparatory agricultural activities are ongoing. Improved rainfall is anticipated from June 18 onwards in some regions. A heatwave is also expected in Vidarbha.

articles.title: India’s gold hunger stays unshaken as higher duties fail to close the import floodgates
articles.summary: India's insatiable thirst for gold remains resilient even in the face of increased import duties. Prior to the tax increase, there was a notable spike in demand, which has since sustained momentum. This pattern implies that heightened taxation is failing to temper the nation’s gold obsession.

This effort involves 380 Quantum Innovation Cells across engineering colleges.

articles.title: Meet Alankrita Pandey, Bengaluru techie-turned-IAS officer now known as 'Bihar's Lady Singham'
articles.summary: The Bihar government has appointed Alankrita Pandey as the new District Magistrate of Bhagalpur. She will replace Dr Naval Kishore Chaudhary in the district. This appointment is a significant government action that will impact the administration of Bhagalpur.
articles.raw_summary: The Bihar government has appointed the 2016-batch IAS officer as the new District Magistrate of Bhagalpur as part of a wider bureaucratic reshuffle. She will replace Dr Naval Kishore Chaudhary in the district.

articles.title: India's macro outlook improving, but IT recovery may be couple of quarters away: Neeraj Seth
articles.summary: Neeraj Seth, Founder and CIO of 3R Investment Management, expects financial stocks to benefit from credit growth and corporate capex, while global AI-driven flows may limit a strong return of foreign investors. Seth remains cautious on IT services in the near term, sees long-term value in gold and expects export-oriented sectors such as textiles to gain from trade agreements and currency trends.

articles.title: Heavy rain to lash Assam, Meghalaya; Delhi braces for dust storm and showers
articles.summary: In southern India, widespread rainfall is expected over Kerala, Coastal Karnataka and Lakshadweep. Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal are likely to receive widespread rainfall on Friday.
articles.raw_summary: In southern India, widespread rainfall is expected over Kerala, Coastal Karnataka and Lakshadweep. Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karaikal are likely to receive widespread rainfall on Friday.

articles.title: TN govt to launch WhatsApp pilot for OPD bookings, expedite Madurai AIIMS: Health Minister
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Health Minister Arun Raj announced a new pilot project to reduce wait times at major state-run hospitals using WhatsApp for OPD registrations. This move aims to improve healthcare services in the state. The pilot project will be launched to expedite the establishment of the Madurai AIIMS.
articles.raw_summary: Tamil Nadu Health Minister Arun Raj on Thursday announced a new pilot project leveraging WhatsApp for OPD registrations to reduce severe wait times at major staterun hospitals like MMC and Stanley.

articles.title: Turtlemint bets on smaller towns as India's insurance market expands
articles.summary: Insurtech firm Turtlemint says smaller towns are becoming the biggest growth driver for insurance in India. CEO Dhirendra Mahyavanshi says the company has issued over 2 crore policies, improved profitability, and expects insurance adoption to rise further with industry reforms and the proposed Bima Sugam platform.

articles.title: Eight indigenous Ladakh crafts get GI tag in recognition of region's distinct cultural heritage
articles.summary: The Indian government has given a GI (Geographical Indication) tag to eight traditional crafts from Ladakh. This recognition aims to preserve and promote the region's unique cultural heritage. The GI tag will help protect the crafts from imitation and ensure their authenticity.
articles.raw_summary: The newly-tagged products are Ladakh Challi Textiles, Ladakh Thigma, Ladakh Painting, Pabu of Ladakh, Ladakh Nambu Textile, Ladakh Pashmina Textile, Ladakh Chilling Metal Work and Ladakh Likir Pottery, officials said.

articles.title: Turtlemint Fintech raises ₹397 crore from anchor investors ahead of IPO launch
articles.summary: The company allotted 2.61 crore equity shares to 32 anchor investors at a face value of ₹1 per share, including a share premium of ₹151 per share.
articles.raw_summary: The company allotted 2.61 crore equity shares to 32 anchor investors at a face value of ₹1 per share, including a share premium of ₹151 per share.

articles.summary: RBI to hold a three day variable rate repo auction of ₹1 lakh crore on June 19 to ease tightening liquidity, after recent large VRR infusions amid narrowing surplus.
articles.raw_summary: RBI to hold a three day variable rate repo auction of ₹1 lakh crore on June 19 to ease tightening liquidity, after recent large VRR infusions amid narrowing surplus.

articles.raw_summary: Shaily Engineering Plastics witnessed a ₹105.4 crore block deal on June 18, with promoter Vanita Nagda selling 4 lakh shares at an average price of ₹2,635.05 each.

articles.title: India is losing gas worth crores every year - and most of it is fixable
articles.summary: Methane emissions from oil and gas operations alone could put nearly 100 bcm of gas back into the market every year if captured, with another 100 bcm available if routine flaring were ended. That is gas already being produced. We are simply letting it escape.

articles.title: Direct tax collections rise 14.6% to ₹5.21 lakh crore till June 17; advance tax jumps 15%
articles.summary: India net direct tax collections rose 14.6 percent to 5.21 lakh crore by June 17 in FY27, led by strong corporate tax and STT growth, indicating healthy tax buoyancy
articles.raw_summary: India net direct tax collections rose 14.6 percent to 5.21 lakh crore by June 17 in FY27, led by strong corporate tax and STT growth, indicating healthy tax buoyancy

articles.title: Karo Sambhav raises ₹56 crore from Rainmatter to expand critical mineral recovery from e-waste
articles.summary: The fresh capital will be used to scale recycling infrastructure aimed at recovering critical, precious, and high-value materials from end-of-life products, particularly electronic waste.
articles.raw_summary: The fresh capital will be used to scale recycling infrastructure aimed at recovering critical, precious, and high-value materials from end-of-life products, particularly electronic waste.

articles.title: India has plenty of bonds. So why is investing in them still so difficult?

articles.raw_summary: India's corporate bond market has grown to nearly ₹59 trillion, but experts say the bigger challenge is not supply. It's making bonds easier to buy, sell and understand for ordinary investors.

articles.raw_summary: Domestic institutional investors bought equities worth ₹3,517 crore on Thursday, offsetting FII selling and helping benchmark indices extend their winning streak to a fifth consecutive session.

articles.title: MSMEs face growing pressure from dumped imports, trade remedy reforms needed: Report
articles.summary: A report by C-DEP Research has urged the government to take swift action to protect MSME manufacturers from dumped imports. The report cites limited consumer price impact but severe harm in sectors like DASDA. It calls for anti-dumping duties to be imposed to protect domestic industries.
articles.raw_summary: C-DEP Research urges swift DGTR anti-dumping duties to protect MSME manufacturers from dumping, citing limited consumer price impact and severe harm in sectors like DASDA.

articles.title: Donate blood, get VIP darshan: How Omkareshwar Temple's unique scheme is making a difference
articles.summary: The Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple in India has launched a scheme to encourage devotees to donate blood. The initiative aims to help local blood banks build healthy reserves. This effort may lead to policy discussions or actions to improve blood donation and healthcare services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: A unique initiative at the Omkareshwar Jyotirlinga Temple is encouraging devotees to donate blood while helping local blood banks build healthy reserves.

articles.title: From Hormuz to nukes: The biggest questions after the US-Iran agreement
articles.summary: The US and Iran have reached a new agreement, easing fears of a wider conflict. However, questions remain about Tehran's regional influence and the future of the Strait of Hormuz. The agreement may have implications for India's foreign policy and regional security.
articles.raw_summary: The new US-Iran agreement has eased fears of a wider conflict, but questions remain over Tehran's regional influence, the future of the Strait of Hormuz, nuclear enrichment rights and whether the truce can deliver lasting stability in West Asia.

articles.title: India's thermal coal imports drop to 4-year low as green power rises
articles.summary: India thermal coal imports fell 12% to 65 million tons in Jan-May as local output and renewables rose, while power demand and renewable generation hit record highs.
articles.raw_summary: India thermal coal imports fell 12% to 65 million tons in Jan-May as local output and renewables rose, while power demand and renewable generation hit record highs.

articles.title: India will be the biggest driver of global oil demand for the next 25 years: OPEC
articles.summary: India is expected to add 8.1 million barrels per day of oil demand between 2025 and 2050—the highest increase globally—as rising incomes, industrialisation and transport needs outweigh the rapid expansion of renewable energy, according to OPEC's latest long-term outlook.
articles.raw_summary: India is expected to add 8.1 million barrels per day of oil demand between 2025 and 2050—the highest increase globally—as rising incomes, industrialisation and transport needs outweigh the rapid expansion of renewable energy, according to OPEC's latest long-term outlook.

articles.title: SEBI proposes overhaul of margin trading rules as MTF volumes rise
articles.summary: SEBI has proposed a broad revamp of the margin trading framework, including allowing brokers to raise funds through NCDs, increasing exposure limits and raising eligibility norms. The regulator said the review is aimed at strengthening risk management and improving ease of doing business as MTF volumes continue to grow.

articles.summary: The UAE became India's second-largest oil supplier, with imports rising nearly 41% to 942,500 bpd in May, the first full month after the Gulf producer's exit from OPEC, as it boosted supplies from Fujairah, according to the data.

articles.raw_summary: The government plans a scheme offering financial aid to contractors for temporary homes for construction workers, funded by labour cess. It may also mandate durability certificates for buildings and aims to boost quality, speed, and sustainability in construction.

He said India has 27 million developers on GitHub, with more than 2 million joining the platform this year alone.

articles.raw_summary: Hindalco Industries has appointed Kapil Agrawal as CEO (Designate) – Copper with effect from November 1, 2026. He will take over as CEO of the Copper business from March 1, 2027, succeeding Rohit Pathak, who will transition to a new role within the Aditya Birla Group.

She expects companies with existing UK exposure, including SP Apparels, KPR Mill, Wellspun Living and Indo Count, to benefit. Elara Capital also sees Arvind Ltd as a top pick, citing growth opportunities in technical textiles and expanding garment exports.

The increase is being driven by new localisation rules under the Approved List of Cell Manufacturers (ALCM), while manufacturers expand capacity to reduce reliance on imports.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu finally admits its biggest debt problem. But can it afford the solution?

The financial sector is expected to remain positive, but the rally in public sector banks may be nearing its end.

articles.summary: Central banks around the world, including those in emerging markets, plan to increase their gold reserves. articles.raw_summary: World Gold Council survey shows 45% of 74 central banks plan to boost gold reserves, mainly in emerging markets, despite recent price pullback and rising political risk

articles.title: NCB launches voluntary code for chemical industry to curb diversion of precursor chemicals

articles.raw_summary: NCB launches Voluntary Code of Conduct for chemical industry in Ahmedabad, urges firms to tighten monitoring and report suspicious precursor chemical transactions.

articles.title: Ladakh LG approves constitution of Snow Leopard and High-Altitude Nature Conservation Society
articles.summary: The LG of Ladakh has approved the constitution of the Snow Leopard and High-Altitude Nature Conservation Society. The society will be chaired by the LG, with several officials as ex-officio members. This move aims to conserve the snow leopard and its habitat in the region.
articles.raw_summary: The LG of Ladakh will be the chairman of the society, while the chief secretary, Ladakh MP, chief executive councillor, LAHDC, Leh, the chief executive councillor, LAHDC, Kargil, and the principal secretary, Department of Forest, Ecology and Environment, will be the ex-officio members, an official spokesperson said.

articles.title: A single 3.3 kg mango from Madhya Pradesh sold for ₹3,800 — why is it so expensive?
articles.summary: A rare and expensive mango variety from Madhya Pradesh has been sold for ₹3,800. The Noorjahan mango is known for its unusual size and premium demand. Its limited availability and short harvest window contribute to its high price.
articles.raw_summary: Grown in limited quantities in Alirajpur’s Katthiwada region, the Noorjahan mango is known for its unusual size and premium demand. The variety attracts buyers every season due to its jumbo weight, short harvest window and limited availability.

articles.summary: Delhi government will introduce an EMI-based payment model for its ₹473 crore streetlight modernisation project, aimed at replacing around 93,000 streetlights with smart LEDs to cut power costs and improve real-time monitoring, while expected savings of about ₹300 crore over five years will be linked to contractor performance.

This move aims to clarify the legal provisions governing Aadhaar's use.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | What a strong El Nino could mean for India
articles.summary: One of the strongest El Nino events in decades is forecast to intensify through the year, raising the risk of a weak monsoon in India. From crop output and food prices to water supplies and electricity generation, Mint explains the potential impact.

articles.title: Mint Explainer| What would it take for India to create a SpaceX?
articles.summary: SpaceX’s $2.5 trillion valuation is driven by its reusable rocket technology, which slashed launch costs and dominates the global space market. India’s startups, Skyroot and Agnikul, are targeting small-satellite launches, but need capital, scale and consistent execution to build a success story.
